title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Taylor has recently moved to another country and is finding it hard to stay connected with friends back home. Write an email to Taylor giving practical advice on how to maintain long-distance friendships, and sharing your own experience of keeping in touch with people who live far away.

Write your email to Taylor.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Taylor,

I was really sorry to hear you are finding it hard to stay connected — honestly, it is one of the most challenging things about moving somewhere new. But I genuinely believe that with a bit of effort, long-distance friendships can be just as meaningful as those you maintain in person.

The most important thing, in my experience, is consistency. Rather than sending the occasional message, try to schedule regular catch-ups — even a short video call once a fortnight can make an enormous difference. Apps like WhatsApp make it easy to share everyday moments through voice notes and photos, which helps friends feel involved in your daily life even from afar.

I also recommend finding a shared activity you can enjoy remotely — watching the same TV series and discussing it, playing an online game together, or even starting a shared playlist. These small rituals give you something to look forward to and talk about.

Finally, do not underestimate the power of a handwritten letter or postcard — it is a small gesture, but people truly appreciate the effort.

Hang in there — it gets easier, I promise!

Warm wishes,
Morgan

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
Your local community centre has asked you to write a report on how families in your area spend time together, based on a survey you conducted. Include findings about the most common family activities, any challenges families face in spending quality time together, and recommendations for how the community centre could help.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Family Time in the Oakfield Community Area

Introduction
This report presents findings from a survey of 120 local residents regarding how families currently spend time together and what barriers they face in doing so.

Common Family Activities
The most frequently cited activities were shared meals, watching television, and occasional day trips. A smaller proportion of respondents reported engaging in more active pursuits such as sport, outdoor walks, or board games. Several families noted that shared activities had decreased since children reached adolescence.

Challenges Identified
The most significant barriers to quality family time were busy work schedules, financial constraints, and — particularly among families with teenagers — differing interests across generations. Many parents expressed a desire to do more with their children but reported a lack of affordable, suitable options locally.

Recommendations
The community centre should consider introducing low-cost family activity sessions, such as weekend cooking classes, sports taster sessions, or creative workshops suitable for mixed age groups. A regular family games evening would provide a relaxed, sociable environment at minimal expense. Promoting these events through local schools would help ensure broad community awareness and participation.

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a book and film review website:

'Family matters! Write a review of a book or film that explores family relationships. Describe the story briefly, explain what you found most interesting or moving about it, and say whether you would recommend it.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Little Fires Everywhere by Celeste Ng — A Compelling Exploration of Family and Identity

Celeste Ng's second novel is a richly layered exploration of family, class, and the choices parents make in the name of love. Set in the carefully planned community of Shaker Heights, Ohio, it follows two very different families — the privileged Richardsons and the nomadic Warrens — whose lives become dramatically intertwined.

What makes this novel so compelling is the nuance with which Ng portrays each character's motivations. No one is simply right or wrong; every decision stems from understandable, deeply human impulses. The exploration of motherhood in its many forms is particularly thought-provoking, raising questions about sacrifice, control, and what it truly means to put a child's needs first.

The prose is elegant and restrained, with a slow-burn tension that builds expertly to a devastating conclusion. Some readers may find the pace occasionally slow, but the payoff is entirely worth it.

I would highly recommend Little Fires Everywhere to anyone who enjoys character-driven literary fiction that challenges comfortable assumptions about family life.
